task: I need to send a quick message to a teacher about a class issue, and I want it to be short enough to send without dragging it out. Can you help me draft something simple, and if there’s a practical problem with the way I’m handling it, point it out clearly?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — I can help with that.

Here’s a short, polite template you can send:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I wanted to let you know about an issue in class: [briefly describe the issue]. I’m not sure what the best way to handle it is, so I wanted to bring it to your attention. Thanks for your time.**

If you want it even shorter:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I wanted to quickly let you know about a class issue: [issue]. Please let me know if there’s anything I should do. Thanks.**

Practical note: if the issue is something urgent, ongoing, or affecting other students, it’s usually better to be specific and mention when it happened rather than keeping it too vague. If it’s a minor issue, a short message like the above is fine.

If you want, I can make it sound more formal, more casual, or tailor it to the exact issue.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, but that’s still too generic. I need something I can actually send, not a blank template with brackets everywhere. Make it short and a little more direct.

Also, if I’m handling it wrong, say it straight — like if I should tell the teacher in person instead of texting/emailing, or if it needs a specific detail so I don’t sound vague.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, direct version you can send:

**Hi [Teacher’s Name], I wanted to let you know there’s been an issue in class that’s affecting me. I didn’t want to make it a big thing, but I thought you should know. Please let me know if you’d prefer I talk to you about it in person.**

Practical note: if this is something that can be handled in person, that’s usually better than a text/email because it’s easier to explain clearly and less likely to sound vague. Also, if you don’t include at least one concrete detail, the message may come off as too general for the teacher to act on.

If you want, I can make it:
- **more direct**
- **more formal**
- **more specific for a real issue**

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still kinda mushy. I need something I can actually send, not “there’s been an issue” fluff.

Just give me a version like: “Hi Mr./Ms. __, I wanted to let you know __ happened in class today. I’m telling you now because I want it handled properly. Let me know if you want me to explain more in person.”

And yeah — if it’s something small, email is fine. If it’s messy or involves other people, in person is better. Also, don’t make it vague. At least name what happened, or it sounds like I’m trying to dodge saying it.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a cleaner version you can actually send:

**Hi Mr./Ms. [Name], I wanted to let you know that [briefly state what happened] in class today. I’m telling you now because I want it handled properly. Let me know if you want me to explain more in person.**

If you want it even more direct:

**Hi Mr./Ms. [Name], [what happened] happened in class today. I wanted to make you aware so it can be handled properly. Let me know if you want more details in person.**

Practical note, straight up:  
- **Small/simple issue:** email is fine.  
- **Messy issue or involves other people:** talk **in person** if you can.  
- **Don’t leave it vague** — name what happened, even briefly, or it can sound evasive and make it harder for the teacher to respond.

If you want, I can turn it into a one-sentence version for a specific situation.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right shape. Still want the actual sendable line, though — no brackets, no extra options.

Give me one version for a small issue and one for a messy one. Short. Direct. No mush.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are two sendable versions:

**Small issue:**  
Hi Mr./Ms. [Name], I wanted to let you know that [briefly state what happened] in class today. I’m telling you so it can be handled properly.

**Messy issue:**  
Hi Mr./Ms. [Name], [what happened] happened in class today, and I wanted to make you aware so it can be handled properly. I can explain more in person if needed.

Straight practical note: don’t send either one without naming the actual issue, even briefly. If it involves other people or could turn into a bigger problem, in person is usually better than email/text.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, but still not quite there. I said no brackets, no placeholders. I need actual sendable lines, even if you have to make up a generic example.

One for a small issue. One for a messy one. Keep it short and blunt.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are two ready-to-send versions:

**Small issue:**  
Hi Mr. Lee, I wanted to let you know that the class got noisy during the group work today. I’m telling you so it can be handled properly.

**Messy issue:**  
Hi Ms. Carter, there was an argument between a few students during class today, and I wanted to make you aware so it can be handled properly. I can explain more in person if needed.

Straight practical note: if it’s messy or involves other people, in person is better than text/email. If it’s small, email is fine.
